sanskrit: दैवी ह्येषा गुणमयी मम माया दुरत्यया । मामेव ये प्रपद्यन्ते मायामेतां तरन्ति ते ॥
hindiTranslation: क्योंकि मेरी यह गुणमयी दैवी माया बड़ी दुरत्यय है अर्थात् इससे पार पाना बड़ा कठिन है ।  जो केवल मेरे ही शरण होते हैं, वे इस मायाको तर जाते हैं ॥
englishTranslation: My divine illusion, made of the three qualities, is hard to cross. Only those who take refuge in Me go beyond it.
essence: The way beyond the veil is not force, but refuge.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: The forces that shape ordinary life are hard to overcome on your own. Surrendering to Krishna is what carries a person beyond them.
simpleInsight: You do not have to outmuscle confusion. There is relief in letting guidance do what force cannot.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is still frozen, and Krishna is explaining why ordinary effort cannot by itself break the hold of the three qualities. This verse names the obstacle and the way through it: surrender.
modernRelevance: You keep trying to think your way out of the same loop at 1:40 a.m., replaying the conversation, the mistake, the fear. The verse says the exit is not more strain, but a deeper refuge.
translationLiteral: दैवी (divine) / हि (indeed) / एषा (this) / गुणमयी (made of the three qualities) / मम (my) / माया (illusion) / दुरत्यया (hard to cross) / माम् (Me) / एव (alone) / ये (those who) / प्रपद्यन्ते (take refuge) / मायाम् (this illusion) / एताम् (this) / तरन्ति (cross beyond) / ते (they)
insight: It shifts the mind from self-reliance against confusion to surrender as the way through confusion.

quoteVariants.text: The veil is hard to cross. Refuge in Me carries you through.
The hard part is not effort. It is where you place trust.
Refuge in Me crosses every veil.
